description_long: This radio receiver features a rectangular body with a brushed metal top and front panel, accented by wooden side panels that give it a classic, vintage appearance. The front face is equipped with multiple rotary knobs, push buttons, and a large tuning dial on the right, as well as a blue-lit frequency display stretching across the upper section. The top panel has ventilation slits for heat dissipation, while the rear panel is fitted with a variety of input/output ports, including RCA connectors and speaker terminals, indicating its use as an audio receiver or amplifier. The back also features color-coded speaker binding posts for left and right channels, and additional ports for auxiliary connections. The overall build suggests a robust, high-quality construction typical of mid-to-late 20th-century home audio equipment. Its design is both functional and decorative, suitable for placement in living rooms, garages, or entertainment spaces.
description: A vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front, wooden side panels, multiple control knobs, and a blue-lit frequency display. The rear features numerous audio input/output ports and speaker terminals.
description_view_0: The front view shows the brushed metal faceplate, blue-lit frequency display, several rotary knobs, push buttons, and a large tuning dial on the right.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wooden panel and the depth of the receiver, with only the protruding knobs visible at the front.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a black panel with multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker binding posts, and auxiliary connections.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wooden panel and the protruding tuning knob at the front.
